title: Bounded Bodies, JSON, Forms, Multipart Uploads, and Validation · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective bounded request decoding and validation pipeline; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded request decoding and validation pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.
Decode exactly one JSON value, reject trailing content and unknown fields when the contract is closed, and distinguish syntax, type, and size failures.
Separate transport shape validation from domain invariants, normalize only defined values, accumulate safe field errors, and preserve causality internally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 2773 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound declared and actually decoded bodies before accepting structured input; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.
